title: verbena serpyllifolia
intro: Verbena serpyllifolia, also known as thymeleaf verbena, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, white flowers and a fragrance similar to thyme. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils. Consuming thymeleaf verbena is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile addition to herbal remedies.
